topics: Why physical activity makes us feel good
Treating others well and being happy for each other
subject: Physical Education

sections.heading: 🏆 The Quest Begins — Why Physical Activity Matters
sections.body: Welcome, young hero! Your first quest is to discover why moving your body every day is so powerful. When you play, run, or exercise, your body and mind both feel stronger and happier. Imagine sitting at home for 7 whole days — no cricket, no gilli-danda, nothing! You would feel bored, grumpy, and even tired. That is the magic spell of physical activity — it keeps your mood bright and your body ready for any challenge!
keyPoints: Regular physical activity makes you physically stronger and mentally happier
Not playing or moving for even a few days can make you feel dull and stressed
Physical education helps you grow, make friends, and stay healthy

sections.heading: 🧊 Traditional Games — Ice Water and Seven Stones
sections.body: Every hero needs allies! Traditional games like Ice Water and Seven Stones teach you the power of teamwork. In Ice Water, if a friend gets frozen, YOU run to save them — just like helping a teammate in kabaddi. In Seven Stones (also called Pitthu or Lagori), two teams work together using speed, aim, and smart thinking to win. These desi games have been played across India for generations and they build agility, alertness, and friendship all at once!
keyPoints: Ice Water teaches you to support and rescue your teammates — nobody is left behind
Seven Stones (Lagori/Pitthu) builds speed, strategy, and teamwork
Traditional games connect you to India's rich culture and keep you fit

sections.heading: 💪 Aerobic Exercises — Growing Strong Like a Wizard
sections.body: A wizard gains power through practice — and so do you! Aerobic exercises are activities that make your heart beat faster and your lungs breathe deeper for a longer time. Think of dancing to your favourite Bollywood song without stopping — that is aerobic exercise! When you do this regularly, your heart becomes stronger, your lungs work better, and you stop getting tired so quickly. Even climbing stairs at school will feel easier the more you practice!
keyPoints: Aerobic exercises increase your heart rate and breathing, making your heart and lungs stronger
Regular exercise combined with healthy food (like dal, roti, fruits) makes bones and muscles grow strong
The more you exercise, the less you pant or get tired during games

sections.heading: 🪞 Follow Me and Number Game — Helping and Treating Each Other Well
sections.body: A true hero always looks out for others! In the Follow Me game, you copy a friend's movements — like a mirror — which builds trust and brings lots of laughter. The Number Game, inspired by the desi game Rumaal Jhapata, teaches you to compete fairly and respect everyone. Just like in a cricket match, you cheer for good play even from the other team. Remember the golden rule of this realm — treat others exactly the way YOU want to be treated!
keyPoints: Follow Me builds emotional bonds and teaches you to respect each other's moves and ideas
The Number Game teaches fair play, quick thinking, and healthy competition
Treating others well — on the field and off it — makes everyone feel safe and happy

sections.heading: 🌟 Hula Hoop Jump — Be Happy for Each Other
sections.body: The final spell of a true champion is joy — not just for yourself, but for everyone! In Hula Hoop Jump, you race by jumping through hoops and passing them to your teammates. Even if your team does not finish first, you cheer for the winner with a big smile. This is called sportsmanship — appreciating the effort of every player, just like clapping for both teams after a school cricket match. Remember, winning is great, but celebrating together makes everyone a champion!
keyPoints: Hula Hoop Jump builds coordination, teamwork, and the habit of cheering for others
Appreciate effort and hard work, not just the final result or trophy
Being genuinely happy for others — even opponents — is the mark of a true hero

summary: Young hero, you have completed your first quest! In Unit 1, you discovered that physical activity is a powerful spell that keeps your body strong and your mind happy. You explored amazing traditional Indian games like Ice Water and Seven Stones that teach teamwork, agility, and friendship. You unlocked the power of aerobic exercises that strengthen your heart and lungs. Through games like Follow Me and the Number Game, you learned to help each other, compete fairly, and treat everyone with respect. Finally, the Hula Hoop Jump reminded you that the greatest power of all is being happy for others — because in this realm of physical education, everyone grows stronger together!
